title: 9 Best Herbal Remedies for Numbness in Hands - Tinctures for Relief

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Bilobalide is good for numbness in hands because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to improve blood flow and reduce swelling in the nerves, which can help to relieve numbness and tingling sensations.

Quercetin is good for numbness in hands because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and improve blood flow to the hands, which can help restore sensation and relieve numbness.

Ginkgolides in Angelica Sinensis are good for numbness in hands because they improve blood circulation and oxygen supply to the hands, reducing numbness and tingling sensations caused by poor circulation.

Isorhapontigenin is good for numbness in hands because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and relax blood vessels, allowing for improved blood circulation to the hands, which can help alleviate numbness caused by poor circulation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tincture is a natural remedy that can help with numbness in hands. This is because it improves blood flow to the hands and fingers. When blood flow is good, the nerves get the oxygen and nutrients they need to work properly. If blood flow is poor, the nerves can become damaged and cause numbness. Ginkgo biloba tincture contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which are special compounds that help widen blood vessels and improve blood circulation. This means that more oxygen and nutrients can reach the nerves in the hands, helping to reduce numbness and tingling. Ginkgo biloba tincture also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the hands. By taking Ginkgo biloba tincture regularly, some people may find that their numbness in hands improves over time.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Angelica sinensis tincture that helps with numbness in hands, you'll need dried Angelica sinensis roots and a few basic supplies. Start by filling a clean glass jar with 1 part dried Angelica sinensis roots and 2 parts 80-proof vodka or glycerin, making sure the roots are fully submerged in the liquid. Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the roots release their active ingredients into the liquid. This process is called maceration, and it allows the roots to infuse their medicinal properties into the solvent. After the maceration period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Your Angelica sinensis tincture is now ready to use; you can take it orally, following the recommended dosage on the label, to help alleviate numbness in your hands and other areas of your body.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, commonly known as ginger, has been used for centuries to help with various health issues. The tincture made from ginger is particularly good for numbness in the hands due to its anti-inflammatory properties. When we have numbness in the hands, it is often because of poor blood circulation or nerve damage. Ginger tincture helps to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation, which in turn helps to relieve numbness and pain. The tincture can be applied topically or taken orally to get the desired benefits. It works by blocking the production of a chemical called prostaglandin, which is responsible for causing pain and inflammation. This helps to reduce the numbness and pain in the hands, making it easier to perform daily activities. The tincture is also easy to use and can be combined with other natural remedies to enhance its effects.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Angelica sinensis herbal tincture that can help with numbness in hands, start by gathering the necessary ingredients and equipment. You'll need dried Angelica sinensis roots, a clean glass jar with a lid, a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle, and a solvent like vodka or glycerin. Begin by grinding the dried roots into a fine powder using the grinder or mortar and pestle, taking care not to inhale the dust. Next, combine the ground roots with the solvent in the glass jar, making sure the roots are fully covered. Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for two to six weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the solvent extract the active compounds from the roots. After the extraction period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. You can now use the tincture as directed, typically by taking a few drops under the tongue or adding it to water or tea. Always consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you're experiencing persistent numbness or other health issues.

Additionally, capsaicin can help to stimulate the nerves in the hands, making them work more efficiently, which can help to improve sensation and reduce numbness. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Angelica sinensis herbal tincture to help with numbness in hands, first, you'll need to gather a few ingredients. Start by purchasing dried Angelica sinensis roots, which are available at most health food stores or online. Next, you'll need a clean glass jar with a lid, a carrier oil such as vodka or glycerin, and a measuring cup. Combine 1 part of the dried Angelica sinensis roots with 2 parts of the carrier oil in the glass jar. For example, if you're using 1 ounce of the dried roots, add 2 ounces of vodka. Make sure to label the jar with the date and ingredients. Now, store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the roots extract their medicinal properties into the oil. After the extraction period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Your Angelica sinensis tincture is now ready to use - simply take a few drops, 2-3 times a day, to help alleviate numbness in your hands.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Angelica sinensis herbal tinctures that help with numbness in hands, you'll need Angelica sinensis roots, a solvent like vodka or glycerin, and a clean glass jar. First, dry the Angelica sinensis roots thoroughly in the sun or using a food dehydrator. Once dry, chop the roots into small pieces and fill a clean glass jar with them. Then, pour the solvent over the roots, making sure they're completely covered. The general ratio is 1 part Angelica sinensis roots to 2-3 parts solvent. Seal the jar tightly and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two. This process is called maceration, and it allows the herbal properties to infuse into the solvent. After the maceration period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Store the tincture in a cool, dark place and use it as directed. To take the tincture, mix a few drops with water and consume it as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica sinensis tincture is a herbal remedy that may help with numbness in hands. The main ingredient, Angelica sinensis, also known as dong quai, is a plant that's been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries. It's rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can help reduce swelling and pain in the hands, which may be causing the numbness. The tincture may also improve circulation and relax blood vessels, allowing more oxygen and nutrients to reach the hands. This can help reduce numbness and tingling sensations. Additionally, Angelica sinensis has been shown to have a positive effect on nerve function, which can help restore sensation in the hands. Taking Angelica sinensis tincture may also have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can contribute to numbness.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Angelica sinensis herbal tinctures that help with numbness in hands, start by gathering dried Angelica sinensis roots and leaves. Fill the jar about 2/3 full with the dried herbs and pour the solvent over them, making sure the herbs are completely covered. Use a measuring cup to measure out the solvent - a general ratio is 1 part herbs to 2-3 parts solvent. After the infusion time is up, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. intro_desc: If you're experiencing numbness in your hands, it's essential to address the issue promptly. Numbness can be a sign of poor circulation, nerve damage, or underlying health conditions, which can affect your daily activities and quality of life. Herbal tinctures, such as those made from Ginkgo biloba and Zingiber officinale, can be a helpful natural remedy to alleviate numbness in the hands. Ginkgo biloba, also known as the "memory tree," is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties that can improve blood flow and reduce nerve inflammation. This can help to restore sensation and circulation to the affected area. Zingiber officinale, or ginger, has natural anti-inflammatory compounds that can also reduce pain and inflammation, making it an excellent addition to a numbness-relieving tincture. By incorporating these herbs into your daily routine, you can experience improved circulation, reduced pain, and increased sensation in your hands.  Fixing numbness in your hands can have a significant impact on your daily life. Being able to feel and move your hands freely can enable you to perform tasks with ease, from cooking and cleaning to playing musical instruments or typing on your computer. Moreover, addressing numbness can also help to prevent further complications, such as nerve damage or poor wound healing. By taking proactive steps to alleviate numbness, you can regain control over your daily activities and maintain your independence.
supplementary_best: To remedy numbness in hands, a combination of herbal tinctures is more effective than using a single herb. The best combination includes Ginkgo biloba, Rosmarinus officinalis, and Piper nigrum. Ginkgo biloba improves blood flow to the hands by dilating blood vessels and increasing circulation. This helps to reduce numbness and tingling sensations. Rosmarinus officinalis (rosemary) has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that protect nerve cells from damage and promote healthy nerve function. Piper nigrum (black pepper) enhances the absorption of other herbs, allowing them to work more efficiently. By combining these three herbs, you can address the underlying causes of numbness in the hands, improving circulation, reducing inflammation, and promoting nerve health.
supplementary_avoid: If you have numbness in your hands, it's best to avoid herbal tinctures made from Aconitum napellus (monk's hood) and Taxus brevifolia (Pacific yew). Aconitum napellus contains a toxin called aconitine that can cause numbness, pain, and other serious problems. Taking a tincture of Aconitum napellus can increase these effects and make your numbness worse. Taxus brevifolia also contains a toxin called taxine that can cause numbness, muscle weakness, and other serious issues. Using a tincture of Taxus brevifolia can lead to these problems, especially if you have numbness in your hands. You should also avoid tinctures made from Rauvolfia serpentina (Indian snakeroot), which can slow down your heart rate and cause numbness or tingling in your hands and feet. Another herb to avoid is Hyoscyamus niger (black henbane), which can cause numbness, confusion, and other problems. These tinctures are not suitable for people with numbness in their hands, and it's best to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
supplementary_other_remedies: Cold or warm compresses can help with numbness in hands. Applying a cold compress for a few minutes can reduce swelling and ease pain by constricting blood vessels. On the other hand, a warm compress can increase blood flow and relax muscles. This can help numbness caused by poor circulation.  Magnesium-rich Epsom salt baths can also be beneficial. Magnesium deficiency can cause numbness and tingling sensations in the hands, and soaking in Epsom salt can help replenish magnesium levels. Magnesium helps to relax muscles and improve blood flow, which can reduce numbness.  Turmeric, a spice that contains a compound called curcumin, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain. Curcumin can also improve blood flow and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to numbness in the hands.  Other natural remedies like essential oils, such as peppermint or eucalyptus, can help improve circulation and reduce muscle tension. However, it's essential to dilute these oils with a carrier oil before applying them to the skin to avoid any adverse reactions.  Ginger, a root that contains compounds with anti-inflammatory properties, can also help reduce swelling and pain. Ginger can be consumed as a tea, added to food, or taken as a supplement. It can help improve circulation and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to numbness in the hands.  Aromatherapy, specifically inhaling the scent of essential oils, can also help improve circulation and reduce muscle tension. Certain scents, such as peppermint or eucalyptus, can help increase alertness and circulation, which can help reduce numbness in the hands.  Acupuncture can also be beneficial in reducing numbness in the hands. This traditional Chinese medicine technique involves inserting thin needles into specific points on the body to stimulate blood flow and relax muscles. Acupuncture can help improve circulation and reduce pain, which can contribute to numbness in the hands.  Aromatherapy massage using essential oils can also be beneficial. Massaging essential oils into the skin can help improve circulation and reduce muscle tension, which can contribute to numbness in the hands.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who experience numbness in their hands may also have other health issues that can be helped with medicinal herbs. One common problem is Raynaud's disease, a condition where blood flow to the fingers is reduced, leading to cold hands and feet. The herb Ginkgo biloba can help improve blood flow and reduce symptoms.  Another condition associated with numbness in the hands is carpal tunnel syndrome, which occurs when the wrist's median nerve is compressed. The herb Devil's claw (Harpagophytum procumbens) can help reduce inflammation and relieve pressure on the nerve.  Numbness in the hands can also be a sign of peripheral neuropathy, a condition where the nerves in the hands and feet are damaged. The herb Ashwagandha (Withania somnifera) can help reduce stress and promote nerve regeneration.  Some people may also experience numbness in their hands due to vitamin deficiencies, particularly vitamin B12. The herb Astragalus (Astragalus membranaceus) can help stimulate the appetite and improve nutrient absorption, which can help alleviate numbness.  Additionally, numbness in the hands can be caused by high blood pressure, which can damage the nerves and blood vessels. The herb Hawthorn (Crataegus spp.) can help lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health.  Lastly, people with numbness in their hands may also experience symptoms of arthritis, such as joint pain and stiffness. The herb Turmeric (Curcuma longa) contains a compound called curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce joint pain and inflammation.  Note: Before using any herbal remedies, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional to ensure safe use and proper dosing.
